is binding until the conclusion of the hearing at which the attendance of the witness is required.


( F5 ... )


Textual Amendments


F5 Words in rule 24.4 omitted (31.12.2020) by virtue of The Family Procedure Rules 2010 and Court of Protection Rules 2017 (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019 (S.I. 2019/517) , regs. 1(1) , 16(3) (with reg. 25 ) (as amended by S.I. 2020/1493 , regs. 1(1) , 8(2)(d) ); 2020 c. 1 , Sch. 5 para. 1(1)


### Who is to serve a witness summons E+W


24.5. —(1) Subject to paragraph (2), a witness summons is to be served by the party on whose behalf it is issued unless that party indicates in writing, when asking the court to issue the summons, that that party wishes the court to serve it instead.


(2) In proceedings to which Part 14 (procedure for applications in adoption, placement and related proceedings) applies, a witness summons is to be served by the court unless the court directs otherwise.


(3) Where the court is to serve the witness summons, the party on whose behalf it is issued must deposit, in the court office, the money to be paid or offered to the witness under rule24.6.


### Right of witness to travelling expenses and compensation for loss of time E+W


24.6. At the time of service of a witness summons the witness must be offered or paid—


### Evidence by deposition E+W


24.7. —(1) A party may apply for an order for a person to be examined before the hearing takes place.


(2) A person from whom evidence is to be obtained following an order under this rule is referred to as a ‘deponent’ and the evidence is referred to as a ‘deposition’.


(3) An order under this rule is for a deponent to be examined on oath before—


(Rule 24.14 makes provision for the appointment of examiners of the court.)


(4) The order may require the production of any document which the court considers is necessary for the purposes of the examination.

metadata.title: Part 24 — Witnesses, Depositions Generally and Taking of Evidence in Member States of the European Union
